rules.action: Remove ingredient completely. No substitution possible.
rules.note: Zero tolerance. Any dish containing these ingredients is haram.

rules.action: Substitute with: cuka putih (white vinegar), air asam jawa (tamarind water), or apple cider vinegar.
rules.note: Even 'cooking wine' is haram. Tamarind is the Malaysian standard substitute.

rules.action: Verify gelatin source. Use agar-agar powder (plant-based) for Malaysian desserts — it is halal.
rules.note: Agar-agar is always safe. Gelatin from beef must have JAKIM-certified halal cert.

rules.action: Request supplier's JAKIM halal certificate before purchase.
rules.note: All meat suppliers in our directory hold JAKIM certification.

rules.action: Remove completely. Not used in Malaysian cuisine.
rules.note: Extremely rare in Malaysian cooking context.

rules.action: Use separate utensils, cutting boards, and cookware for halal food. Wash with running water.
rules.note: Critical for catering operations serving mixed dietary requirements.
